title: Agentic Investing
author: AffectionateFrame359
selftext:
I've been building this AI agent since last August to hunt for stocks because I didn't want to . It’s been running in a live simulation for about 9 months now and I just checked the trading212 and up **29.9% overall** and literally just jumped **10% in the last 7 days.**

​I’m ranting because I spent months tweaking the logic and I thought it was broken during the flat periods, but it is finally hitting.

# ​The stuff that’s actually carrying the portfolio:

* ​**TeraWulf (WULF) & Applied Digital (APLD):** The bot was obsessed with these "bitcoin miners" back when everyone hated them. It wasn't even looking at the BTC price and it flagged them for their power infrastructure. Now that everyone is thirsting for AI data centres, WULF is up **184%** for me. They just did a massive $1B equity raise this week to pivot their Hawesville site to AI, and the market absolutely ate it up.
* ​**Mercury Systems (MRCY):** This one was a total sleeper. It’s a defence tech play that’s been ugly for ages, but it just beat earnings today (May 6th) by like 350% on record bookings. The bot held through all the boring sideways movement and now it’s up 34**%**.
* ​**Frontline (FRO):** Just a classic tanker play. With the Strait of Hormuz drama and oil prices being weird, shipping rates are staying high and the cash flow is just stupid. Bot caught the trend early.



* ​**NuScale Power (SMR):** Regulatory hell. It’s down **61%**. It bought into the "Small Modular Reactor" hype way too high.
* ​**Genius Sports (GENI):** Down **65%**. I have a limit sell sitting there at $16.00 just waiting for a miracle.

​The agent is still heavily weighted in these "infrastructure for AI" plays (WULF, APLD, RGTI). Part of me feels like I should take the 29% win and run, but the agent's logic is doubling down on the fact that we're still in the "build-out" phase of the cycle.

​Anyone else working on an AI system for their future?

comments.author: SmartWeb2711
comments.body: any AI agent you are using ? can you share repo if you wish

replies.author: Vortextgamer
replies.body: I have been using riplit and used a two ai system with opus and gemini together. Gemini can veto opus in decision-making and opus can push back gemini, and both must agree on each other's logic they both have roles. Gemini I told is the strategic analysis while opus has the role of engineer i told them there roles, told them to also monitor each other for hallucinations. I then spent around 3 weeks building very careful context density with each one and then with each other being careful of any context dilution and once we had a solid foundation used just simple brainstorm session as a group to come up with the features and scope and instructed opus to start building. It's been about a month so far of just building breaking testing but so far its going very well with our alpaca account using API trading linked to riplit

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: I think telling the to monitor each other for hallucinations is futile, better to have steps and data chunked down to smaller steps to stop drift depending on your model.

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: Sorry not sharing the repo as I feel the system could be perhaps sold or licensed one day if I can smooth some stuff out.

replies.author: meshifthenelse
replies.body: Good for you.

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: Thanks! Good and fun project for sure!

comments.author: kofster
comments.body:
Old you share some light on how this works? Is it on a desktop, Mac or windows platform and you have an iPhone companion app where you can make monitor what the agent is doing or even make manual trades? 

Or is it an iPhone application and it has everything built in and you can initiate and set up on the phone?

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: It is hosted on a vps and runs once a week, no app for monitoring yet but I get telegram alerts once the scan is complete, it also outputs formatted documents for each of the companies it surfaces which there are examples of on my profile. As for making it available for purchase it was just a side project but a few folks have asked that, so perhaps one day I could.

comments.author: Vortextgamer
comments.body: This is very interesting. i have been building a bot for AI infastrucutre play only its a niche area in terms of scope but i keep my bot to the Ai Universe because i genuienly belive you can not have a bot thats good for the intire market. it has to have a focus. its funny how your bot is picking up the AI space

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: Yes I am pulling data from Financial Modelling Prep and ranking \~500 companies from that, so yes weird how it is picking up the AI space, AI bias???

comments.author: sheeburashka
comments.body:
Important to remember the relative context here:

Since \~August 2025 S&P is +17.5% including dividends. You’re beating the S&P500.

Tech ETF is +29% over same timeframe.

You mostly built a tech portfolio and you’re getting appropriate returns. Test this strategy when the market isn’t as hot and you very well may have a different outcome.

I do hope you truly have something but this proves very little unfortunately.

P.S. I am also tinkering with this and built an option trading bot. I am up 60% over \~8 days. Feels great but at the end of the day it is hard to lose (of late) in tech in this market. I won’t dump my money into this any time soon.

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body:
Good insight but I didn't build a tech portfolio, my AI agent did. I didn't have a say in any of these stocks, only the company parameters that ended up surfacing around a few hundred companies from scans, then AI decided the top ~50 with the best finance metrics and short term horizon potential, then from those a shortlist was produced. I hear what you are saying about tech ETF being comparable returns but I didn't not hand select any of these, after building the system I pushed one button and they were shown to me.

What are you using to build the option trading bot? Are you doing a RAG system?

replies.author: meshifthenelse
replies.body: Which LLM provider did you use? And LLM models typically lag behind since they train on old data. So how can you trust its decisions?

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body:
Because it is a RAG system and I am feeding live market data to the LLMs. Gemini and GPT are uses, Gemini does news and company info GPT does data analysis for financial health.

Obviously you can never trust the output of an LLM 100% but there are things you can do, such as RAG which is retrieval augmented generation, rather than them scanning the internet for stuff they read the data silo.

comments.author: Noah_saav
comments.body: In this market that’s actually not that good. I’m up 50% over the month and feel like I could be doing better

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: But how much time are you spending? I made this system to save me research time and provide insights direct to my inbox. It has beaten the s&p since august 2025 so I'd argue for an automated system it is doing alright. All I need to do now is push one button and I get a shortlist of stocks every week in a nicely formatted 10-20 page document, for me it is not about the gains it is about saving time and having a system on autopilot. I can invest outside my ISA and SIPP with the click of a button how about you?

replies.author: Noah_saav
replies.body:
Not that much at all, I don’t over trade at all actually. I’ve done maybe 12 buys and 3 rebalances over last 2 months. 

All AI related stocks have beat the S&P. A lot by a very wide margin. This portfolio compared to s&p is a totally different risk return profile. 

The fact that you have some 60% losses in this market is cause to see that this isn’t any edge.

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: Didn't say it's an edge, it's a hobby project I've built and yes some are -60% but the majority are winners and some hovering around+200%. In this folio the winners outweigh the losers so it's nothing to scoff at. Keep following and see how V2 goes ;).

replies.author: Noah_saav
replies.body: Sound like you’re over trading the portfolio if it’s only up 29% over 9 months. You should do attribution analysis on this

replies.author: AffectionateFrame359
replies.body: Not over trading at all, done 3 scans with the system and haven't changed allocations since August. What attribution analysis tools would you recommend?

replies.author: Noah_saav
replies.body: [https://rpc.cfainstitute.org/topics/performance-attribution](https://rpc.cfainstitute.org/topics/performance-attribution)

comments.author: Menu-Quirky
comments.body:
Built the first agentic AI task for my brokerage , Let me know of something is wrong

The agent is set up and ready to go. Here's a quick summary of what it will do:

Monitors NVDA every 5 minutes during market hours, daily

Triggers if NVDA drops 1% or more from the previous close

Checks there are no existing short NVDA puts in account

Checks buying power is $50,000 or more

Sells 1 put contract at the nearest weekly expiration, closest strike to 5% below the current price at time of trigger

Runs until it executes once per day, resetting each trading day

it sold one Contract today on forst day , Details: Asset NVDA Type Put Strike Price 215

Expiration Jun 03, 2026 Order Details: Price $0.32
